title: Wire pipeline_error_handler into the orchestrator — the execution log exists but no pipeline run has ever written to it
description: pipeline_error_handler.Notebook is a complete 521-line implementation (error categorization against transient/permanent pattern lists, gold_pipeline_execution_log schema + ensure_log_table_exists, log_activity_start/success/failure, get_execution_summary, failure reporting, get_retry_effectiveness) that nothing calls. It is not an activity in orchestrator_pipeline_bronze_to_gold, no notebook %run's it, and gold_pipeline_execution_log is referenced by no other artifact in fabric/ — so the table is never populated by an orchestrated run and every reporting function queries an empty table. All 8 pipeline activities depend on their predecessors with dependencyConditions ['Succeeded'] only; there is no Failed branch anywhere in the pipeline, so no failure path exists to log from. Retry policies ARE configured and do work (retry 3/3/2/3/2/2/2/1; empirically confirmed when data_quality_checks with retry=1 produced two attempts that failed identically), so what is missing is observability of failures, not resilience against them. Closing this is a precondition for task-037, whose criteria assert that a failure is written to the execution log and that a notification fires.
acceptanceCriteria: The failure-propagation topology is DECIDED and recorded before implementation. SETTLED BY DEC-004 (decided 2026-07-23): Option A — pipeline-level on-failure activities using dependencyConditions ['Failed', 'Skipped'], fanned in on the terminal activity plus a trailing Fail activity: 2 activities total, NOT 8. This criterion originally warned that 'Fabric ANDs multiple dependsOn entries on one activity, so guarding N activities is not simply one handler with N dependencies'. The AND premise is correct and Fabric-doc-confirmed, but the inference was wrong for THIS pipeline: it is a single convergent DAG in which all 8 activities are ancestors of data_quality_checks, and 'Skipped' propagates transitively — so the documented 'Generic error handling' fan-in covers all 8 with one handler. Implementation must read per-activity outcomes via POST queryactivityruns, NOT @activity('X').Error, because skipped activities carry no error field. Revisit only if the DAG stops being singly-terminal: a tail fork would silently un-cover the new branch. (Closes FR-015.)

A failure in at least one Copy or RefreshDataflow activity (i.e. a non-notebook activity) results in a row in gold_pipeline_execution_log. This is the coverage case option B cannot satisfy and is the reason the topology decision matters — do not let the criterion collapse to notebook-only logging without an explicit, recorded scope reduction.

A successful orchestrated run also populates the log (log_activity_start / log_activity_success), so get_retry_effectiveness and get_execution_summary have both outcomes to compare rather than only failures.

error_category is populated from categorize_error() on logged failures, and at least one real failure is confirmed to land in a sensible category — the transient/permanent pattern lists have never been exercised against a live Fabric error string.

The notification sink is configured or explicitly deferred with a reason. All three RefreshDataflow activities currently carry "NoNotification" and the pipeline JSON contains no email/Teams/webhook configuration of any kind, so task-037's notification criterion has nothing to exercise today. Deferring is acceptable; leaving it undecided is not.

[Erik/Fabric] Deployed and demonstrated end-to-end: induce a failure, confirm the log row appears with a usable error message, timestamp, retry_attempt and pipeline_run_id, then confirm a clean run logs its successes.
task-037's status is reconciled once this lands — it is currently Pending with criteria that cannot pass, and should either be unblocked or have its dependency on this task recorded.
notes: CREATED 2026-07-22, closing a loose end from task-026 criterion 7. That criterion required the pipeline_error_handler decision be RECORDED (it was: 'spun into its own task, not wired here, not silently deferred'), but the promised task was never created. task-026 is Finished on its own terms; this is the follow-through.

VERIFIED UNREFERENCED (with a positive control, per rules/agents.md § Negative Findings): grep for 'pipeline_error_handler' across fabric/ returns only its own .platform file; grep for 'gold_pipeline_execution_log' returns only the handler notebook plus two documents (error_recovery_playbook.md, error_handling_strategy.md). Control probe for 'silver_procurement' over the same tree returned 3 files, so the search works and the absence is real rather than a failed probe.

PIPELINE STATE AT CREATION: 8 activities — bronzecopy_EUSupplyShares, bronzecopy_GlobalSupplyShares (Copy); bronze_WGI, bronze_procurement, bronze_EPI (RefreshDataflow); bronze-to-silver data cleaning, silver-to-gold, data_quality_checks (TridentNotebook). Every dependsOn uses dependencyConditions ['Succeeded']. Retry policies: 3, 3, 2, 3, 2, 2, 2, 1.

WHAT IS NOT BROKEN: retry logic works and is proven — data_quality_checks with retry=1 produced exactly 2 attempts on the 2026-07-22 18:41 and 20:16 runs, both failing identically. task-011 delivered real resilience. The gap is purely that failures leave no durable trace, so there is no way to answer 'how often does this pipeline fail, on what, and do retries rescue it'.

BLOCKS task-037 ('Test pipeline error handling and retry logic with simulated failures'): its criterion 2 asserts the failure is written to the execution/error log table, and criterion 3 asserts a notification fires. Neither can pass today — nothing writes the table and notifications are off. task-037 is Pending and looks routable, which is misleading; record the dependency when this task is picked up.

RELATED to task-040 (DQ gate verdict not persisted). Both are the same class of defect — the pipeline's observability artifacts exist but are not connected to the runs they describe — but they are separate surfaces: 040 covers gold_quality_history and the DQ gate's outcome, this covers gold_pipeline_execution_log and activity-level failures. Do not merge them; a single run can pass every DQ check and still fail at a Copy activity.

DESIGN CONTEXT from task-026's deferral rationale: this needs a failure-propagation topology decision AND a notification sink Erik configures, which is why it was kept out of 026 rather than bundled — wiring it there would have conflated the DQ gate with general pipeline error handling.

SCOPE BOUNDARY: this task does not change retry counts, does not alter the DQ gate, and does not add new error categories. It connects an existing, complete implementation to the pipeline that should be feeding it.

GATED ON DEC-004 (2026-07-23): criterion 1's failure-propagation topology was filed as a decision record (.claude/support/decisions/decision-004-pipeline-failure-propagation-topology.md) rather than decided inline, per criterion 1's own instruction and rules/decisions.md. research-agent is populating its four open questions: (1) does Fabric AND multiple dependsOn entries on one activity — decides whether Option A costs 1 handler or 8; (2) can a parent pipeline recover WHICH child activity failed — decides whether Option C is usable; (3) what failure context is reachable from @activity()/@pipeline() system variables — log_activity_failure needs activity name, error message, pipeline_run_id and retry attempt; (4) what notification sink is actually available (criterion 5). Do not start implementation until Erik ticks a box.

DEC-004 RESEARCHED 2026-07-23 — and it OVERTURNS this task's criterion 1. The AND premise is correct (now Fabric-doc-confirmed on the Outlook activity page), but the cost inference is wrong for THIS pipeline: the 8 activities form a single convergent DAG whose only terminal node is data_quality_checks, and Skipped propagates transitively, so the documented 'Generic error handling' fan-in — one handler on the last activity via BOTH Failed and Skipped — guards all 8. Option A costs 2 activities (handler + Fail), not 8. Criterion 1's '1 vs 8' cost framing should be corrected via /iterate when the decision lands. Tracked as FR-015.

TRAP found by research, not previously recorded anywhere: attaching an on-failure branch to the terminal activity produces the documented Try-Catch shape, which makes the overall pipeline run report SUCCESS when the handler succeeds. That would silently undo task-026's DQ gate — every failing run would go green. A trailing native Fail activity on the handler's Succeeded path is REQUIRED, not optional. No Fail/Invoke/Web/Outlook activity exists in fabric/ today (verified with a positive control).

TWO PREREQUISITES this task's cost model omitted, applying to Options A and C alike: (1) pipeline_error_handler has NO parameter cell, and parameter cells are a Fabric-UI toggle that cannot be hand-authored in notebook-content.py — Erik must toggle one before any parameter reaches the handler, which makes this task owner:both on the Fabric side regardless of option. (2) The handler's logging API is two-phase (log_activity_start returns a UUID that log_activity_failure UPDATEs); a pipeline-level failure branch has no start call to pair with, so a one-shot log_activity_outcome(...) must be added. Option B is the only option whose API fits as written.

CRITERION 5 CAN BE ANSWERED, NOT DEFERRED: four real sinks found. Cheapest credible is scheduled-run Failure notifications (Home > Schedule > Failure notifications) — zero pipeline JSON, zero CI/CD exposure — whose one documented gap is 'notifications aren't sent for on-demand runs', exactly what task-037's induced-failure test would be. Pair with an Activator rule on pipeline job events to close it. The Outlook/Teams activity is the only sink that can show the actual error text but is documented 'inactive when using CI/CD' — a re-binding chore on every deploy.

EXPERIMENT REQUIRED BEFORE IMPLEMENTATION (~30 min, throwaway artifacts): does queryactivityruns return rows for a run that is STILL IN PROGRESS? The handler executes inside the run it would query and the docs do not address it. Scratch pipeline: Wait(2s) -> a deliberately-failing activity -> a scratch notebook on ["Failed","Skipped"] that POSTs queryactivityruns with @pipeline().RunId and prints the raw response. Rows present -> recommended shape confirmed. Empty/404 -> fall back to Option A's 8-handler form with literal names and @activity('X').Error.Message. 401/403 -> the notebookutils getToken('pbi') scope caveat bit; needs MSAL or a workspace-identity connection.

FR-015 CLOSED 2026-07-23: criterion 1's cost framing (1 handler vs 8) was corrected in place once DEC-004 landed. The friction was captured while the decision was still open; it recorded that the criterion's AND-premise was right but its conclusion wrong for a singly-terminal DAG. Criterion 1 now states the settled topology, the queryactivityruns mechanism, and the single condition under which the choice should be revisited.

FR-014 ASSIGNED 2026-07-23: error_recovery_playbook.md documents a stale Fabric UI path for failure email ('Settings gear > Notifications'; the current surface is Home > Schedule > Failure notifications) and omits that notifications do not fire for on-demand runs. It also asserts email 'cannot be set via the pipeline JSON alone' while overlooking that the three RefreshDataflow activities already carry a JSON-settable notifyOption that could be MailOnFailure. This lands in THIS task because criterion 5 already requires the notification sink to be configured or explicitly deferred — fix the playbook as part of resolving that criterion.

test_protocol.summary: Prove the execution log receives both outcomes from a real orchestrated run.

steps.instruction: Induce a failure in a non-notebook activity (e.g. point a Copy activity at a bad URL, or unbind a dataflow connection) and run the pipeline
steps.expected: gold_pipeline_execution_log gains a FAILED row with error_message, timestamp, retry_attempt and pipeline_run_id; error_category is populated by categorize_error()

steps.instruction: Run the pipeline clean, then call get_execution_summary() and get_retry_effectiveness()
steps.expected: Both return populated results rather than empty frames — success rows are logged too, not only failures

steps.instruction: Confirm the notification decision: either a notification is delivered, or the deferral is recorded in the task notes with a reason
steps.expected: No silent gap — task-037's notification criterion has either something to test or a documented reason it does not
